─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Intake and Triage Workflow ]─ ┐

Intake and Triage Workflow

GitHub Actions workflow in the lore source repo that fires on field-fix issues and PRs. Uses anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 for AI-powered classification and duplicate detection.

Goal

Incoming field-fix submissions are automatically classified, labeled, and checked for duplicates — reducing human triage burden to final review and merge decisions.

Pipeline

On issue creation (field-fix template)

  1. Parse metadata — Extract structured fields from the issue form
  2. Classify — Determine affected component (hooks, scripts, lib, skills), severity (critical/moderate/low), platform scope (single/multi)
  3. Auto-label — Apply labels: field-fix, component label, severity label, platform label
  4. Duplicate scan — Search open issues and PRs for semantic similarity (title + root cause description)
  5. If duplicate found — Comment linking related issues, suggest grouping under a parent issue

On PR creation (field-fix template)

  1. Parse YAML metadata — Extract structured block from PR body
  2. Validate completeness — Check all required schema fields are present
  3. Cross-reference issue — Verify linked issue exists and is a field-fix issue
  4. Classify and label — Same classification as issues, applied to the PR
  5. Trigger AI review gate (Phase 4) — If the review workflow exists, trigger it

Tools

  • anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 — AI classification and duplicate detection
  • GitHub Actions — Workflow orchestration, labeling, commenting
  • GitHub API — Issue/PR search for duplicate detection

Scope

Target repo: /home/andrew/Github/lore (source framework)

Files to create:

  • .github/workflows/field-fix-triage.yml — the Actions workflow
  • .github/prompts/field-fix-classify.md — prompt template for Claude Code Action classification
  • .github/prompts/field-fix-duplicate.md — prompt template for duplicate detection

Triggers:

  • issues: [opened] with field-fix template
  • pull_request: [opened] with field-fix in title or label

Duplicate Detection Approach

Start with a pragmatic heuristic approach (MVP), with a path to semantic embeddings in Phase 5:

  1. Title similarity — Levenshtein or token overlap against open field-fix issues
  2. Component match — Same affected component narrows the candidate set
  3. AI comparison — Claude Code Action compares root cause descriptions of candidate matches and determines if they describe the same underlying bug
  4. Threshold — If AI confidence > 80%, comment with duplicate link. If 50-80%, comment as “possibly related.”

Dependencies

  • Phase 1 (metadata format) — workflow parses the schema defined there

Deliverables

  • GitHub Actions workflow fires on field-fix issues and PRs
  • Classification produces correct component/severity/platform labels
  • Duplicate detection links related issues when overlap exists
  • Incomplete metadata triggers a comment requesting missing fields
  • Workflow runs in under 2 minutes for typical submissions

Success Criteria

  • A field-fix issue filed via the template gets auto-labeled within 5 minutes
  • A field-fix PR with valid metadata gets classified and cross-referenced
  • Known duplicates (same root cause, different wording) are detected and linked
  • Invalid or incomplete submissions get a clear comment explaining what’s missing
  • Workflow doesn’t fire on non-field-fix issues or PRs

Test Plan

# Create a test field-fix issue via gh CLI and verify labels appear
gh issue create --template field-fix.yml --title "Test: hook fails on macOS" --body "..."

# Create a second issue with similar root cause and verify duplicate detection
gh issue create --template field-fix.yml --title "Test: hook error on Mac" --body "..."

# Verify workflow logs in Actions tab
gh run list --workflow=field-fix-triage.yml
